svg.text: TURKISH
HISTORY & ANATOLIA
TURKISH ORIGINS
Turkic languages: origin in Central Asian steppes ~500 BCE
1071 CE: Battle of Manzikert — Seljuk Turks enter Anatolia
1299: Ottoman Sultanate founded by Osman I
1453: Ottoman conquest of Constantinople (now Istanbul)
1517: Ottoman control of Arab world; Arabic loanwords pour into Turkish
REPUBLIC OF TURKEY & LANGUAGE REFORM
1923: Turkish Republic founded by Mustafa Kemal Atatürk
1928: Latin alphabet replaces Ottoman Arabic script
1932: Turkish Language Association purges Arabic/Persian loanwords
~80M speakers; official in Turkey and Northern Cyprus
KURDISH HISTORY · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
KURDISH ORIGINS
Kurdish: descended from Old Iranian (same root as Persian)
Medes (ancient Iran): possible ancestors of Kurdish people
Kurdistan: "Land of the Kurds" — no independent state exists
Kurdish territory spans Turkey, Iraq, Syria, Iran
OTTOMAN KURDISH RELATIONS
Kurdish emirs: semi-autonomous under Ottoman system
1514: Ottomans ally with Kurds against Safavid Persia
1880: Sheikh Ubeydullah revolt: first modern Kurdish nationalist uprising
1920: Treaty of Sèvres promised Kurdish autonomy (never implemented)
KURDISH IN TURKEY
~20M Kurds in Turkey: largest ethnic minority (~25% of population)
1924-1991: Kurdish language banned in Turkey
1984: PKK insurgency begins; language debate intensifies
1991: Kurdish language restrictions eased; 2003: private Kurdish TV allowed
TWO LANGUAGES, ONE MOUNTAIN
Both peoples: built by the same Anatolian/Mesopotamian landscape
Turkish: Altaic steppe language transplanted to ancient crossroads
Kurdish: Iranian mountain language of the ancient Near East
Anatolia has been home to both for nearly a thousand years
PLATE 51d ALT · TURKISH / KURDISH · HISTORY
